column1: # Twilight Struggle canonical card data - Deluxe Edition (including optional promo cards)
# Sources: GMT Games Twilight Struggle Deluxe Edition rulebook and card backs;
#   ITS (International Twilight Struggle) competitive rules;
#   BGG entry #12333 card list.
# card_id assignment:
#   1-35   Early War (35 cards: 3 scoring + 32 event cards)
#   36-103 Mid War (68 cards: 4 scoring + 64 event cards)
#          NOTE: Mid War block is large because the standard deck has ~46 Mid War
#          cards. The range 36-103 is padded; actual card ids follow physical
#          card numbering where known. Unconfirmed ids are marked with a comment.
#   104-110 Late War (7 cards: 0 scoring + 7 event cards in base; Deluxe adds more)
#   6      China Card (NOT in draw deck; special card passed between players)
# The Deluxe Edition has exactly 110 standard cards plus the China Card.
# The 4 optional/promo cards (Cambridge Five
# Defectors) ARE included in ITS competitive play and are counted in the 110.
# starred: true = event fires once then card is removed from game permanently.
# is_scoring: true = MUST be played as event; cannot use for ops
#   hold past hand limit. Has ops=0.
# prevents_held_after_turn: if non-empty integer N
#   at end of turn N is illegal (house/competitive rule: opponent wins if you
#   hold a scoring card past the end of its era's final turn).
#   Early War scoring cards: turn 3. Mid War scoring cards: turn 6.
#   Late War scoring cards: no cross-era holdover possible (turn 10 is game end).
#   Southeast Asia Scoring is starred
column2: so once played it is removed; no holdover.
